questionText: A particle moves along a curved path given by $x = 2t$ and $y = t^{2} - 3t$. Find the gradient of the path when $t = 1$, and state whether the path is rising or falling there.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiating $x = 2t$ gives the rate at which the horizontal position changes with time.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiating $y = t^{2} - 3t$ gives the rate at which the height changes with time.
The gradient of the path is the vertical rate divided by the horizontal rate, which combines the two using the chain rule.
Putting in $t = 1$ gives the numerical value of the gradient at that point on the path.
A negative gradient means that as the particle moves to the right its height is decreasing, so the path slopes downward.
So at $t = 1$ the gradient is $-\tfrac{1}{2}$ and the path is falling there.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: At $t = 1$ the gradient of the path is $-\dfrac{1}{2}$, so the path is falling there.
